The **2016 [Russian Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "Russian Figure Skating Championships")** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Чемпионат России по фигурному катанию на коньках 2016) were held from 23 to 27 December 2015 in [Yekaterinburg](/wiki/Yekaterinburg "Yekaterinburg"), [Sverdlovsk Oblast](/wiki/Sverdlovsk_Oblast "Sverdlovsk Oblast"). Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dancing](/wiki/Ice_dancing "Ice dancing"). The results were part of the selection criteria for Russia's teams sent to the [2016 World Championships](/wiki/2016_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2016 World Figure Skating Championships") and [2016 European Championships](/wiki/2016_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2016 European Figure Skating Championships").

## Competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2016_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Competitions")]

In the 2015–16 season, Russian skaters competed in domestic qualifying events and national championships for various age levels. The Russian Cup series led to three events – the Russian Championships, the Russian Junior Championships, and the Russian Cup Final.

## Senior Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2016_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Senior Championships")]

The senior Championships were held in [Yekaterinburg](/wiki/Yekaterinburg "Yekaterinburg") from 23 to 27 December 2015.[[1]](#cite_note-calendar1-1) Competitors qualified through international success or by competing in the Russian Cup series' senior-level events. The list of competitors was published on 23 December.[[2]](#cite_note-Entries-2) [Ksenia Stolbova](/wiki/Ksenia_Stolbova "Ksenia Stolbova") / [Fedor Klimov](/wiki/Fedor_Klimov "Fedor Klimov") (due to Klimov's back pain) and [Ksenia Monko](/wiki/Ksenia_Monko "Ksenia Monko") / [Kirill Khaliavin](/wiki/Kirill_Khaliavin "Kirill Khaliavin") (due to Monko's injury sustained at the [2015 Rostelecom Cup](/wiki/2015_Rostelecom_Cup "2015 Rostelecom Cup")) withdrew before the start of the competition.[[3]](#cite_note-FSR1622-3)

## Junior Championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2016_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Junior Championships")]

The 2016 [Russian Junior Championships](/wiki/Russian_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "Russian Junior Figure Skating Championships") ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Первенство России среди юниоров 2016) were held in [Chelyabinsk](/wiki/Chelyabinsk "Chelyabinsk") from 21 to 23 January 2016.[[4]](#cite_note-calendar2-4) Competitors qualified by competing in the Russian Cup series' junior-level events. The results of the January competition were part of the selection criteria for the [2016 Winter Youth Olympics](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2016_Winter_Youth_Olympics "Figure skating at the 2016 Winter Youth Olympics") and the [2016 World Junior Championships](/wiki/2016_World_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "2016 World Junior Figure Skating Championships"). The list of qualifiers was published on 12 January 2016.[[5]](#cite_note-EntriesJ-5) [Alena Kostornaia](/wiki/Alena_Kostornaia "Alena Kostornaia") withdrew before the start of the event and was replaced by Elizaveta Iushenko.[[5]](#cite_note-EntriesJ-5)
